name: gherkin-official
documentation:
# Gherkin for Python

Gherkin parser/compiler for Python. Please see [Gherkin](https://github.com/cucumber/gherkin) for details.

classes.name: gherkin.token_scanner.TokenScanner
documentation:
The scanner reads a gherkin doc (typically read from a `.feature` file) and creates a token for
each line.

The tokens are passed to the parser, which outputs an AST (Abstract Syntax Tree).

If the scanner sees a `#` language header, it will reconfigure itself dynamically to look for
Gherkin keywords for the associated language. The keywords are defined in
{file}`gherkin-languages.json`.

methods.name: gherkin.gherkin_line.GherkinLine.split_table_cells
documentation:
An iterator returning all the table cells in a row with their positions,
accounting for escaping.

returns.name:
(
    Step
    | DocString
    | DataTable
    | Background
    | Scenario
    | Examples
    | list[TableRow]
    | str
    | None
    | Rule
    | Feature
    | GherkinDocument
    | AstNode
)

methods.name: gherkin.token_matcher_markdown.GherkinInMarkdownTokenMatcher.match_Language
documentation: We've made a deliberate choice not to support `# language: [ISO 639-1]` headers or similar
in Markdown. Users should specify a language globally.

modules.name: gherkin.parser
documentation: This file is generated. Do not edit! Edit gherkin-python.razor instead.
